mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0 percent, labor market tightness signals strong consumer demand and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero when UNRATE remains below 4.0 percent for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and residential investment demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are not significantly higher when both series show positive three-month momentum compared to when only one series expands.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 105 signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which historically precedes positive equity returns over a 20-day window during post-shock economic recoveries.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or statistically insignificant when INDPRO remains above 105 for two consecutive months during post-shock periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical median trend reduces earnings recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window despite positive GDP surprises relative to consensus forecasts.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation above 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive or neutral when monthly CPI growth exceeds 0.4 percent for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong non-farm payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market healing, which historically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon during early-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly higher when PAYEMS and UNRATE move in the specified directions simultaneously versus when they diverge.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts consistently above 1.3 million units signal sustained residential investment and consumer confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter when interest rates remain low.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or statistically insignificant when HOUST exceeds 1300 but FEDFUNDS rises above 2.5 percent concurrently.
